codeReview.summary: Reviewed the original failed feature (`integration-mobile-runtime-sync-and-safe-affordances-fix`, commit `33425a4`) together with the current fix feature (`mobile-app-fix-notification-delivery-preferences`, commit `04025a2`) and the intervening branch-target fix now at HEAD (`8c32448`). The original branch-switch approval-target failure is resolved: bridge approvals now carry the exact branch and the mobile presenter renders it. The new notification work does introduce a live-stream-backed delivery/suppression path controlled by the settings toggles, so this is no longer only local UI state. However, two blocking gaps remain: runtime notifications are only surfaced while ThreadListPage/ThreadDetailPage is the current route, and persisted disabled preferences can still leak notifications during app startup before async preference restore completes.

issues.description: The new delivery path is still not app-wide. `runtimeNotificationDeliveryControllerProvider` is only consumed from `ThreadListPage` and `ThreadDetailPage` (`thread_list_page.dart:49-57`, `thread_detail_page.dart:66-74`), and both handlers immediately return whenever their route is not current (`thread_list_page.dart:205-211`, `thread_detail_page.dart:151-157`). But Settings/Approvals are pushed on top of those routes (`thread_list_page.dart:84-87,105-107`; `thread_detail_page.dart:100-102`), and no other page consumes the controller. If an approval or live-activity event arrives while the user is on Settings or Approvals Queue, it stays stranded in `pendingNotifications` until some later provider change occurs, so runtime delivery is still unreliable in normal navigation flows.

Persisted disabled preferences are not fully honored on relaunch/cold start. The provider seeds the controller from `ref.read(notificationPreferencesControllerProvider)` and starts the live subscription immediately (`runtime_notification_delivery_controller.dart:18,94-100`), but `NotificationPreferencesController` defaults both toggles to `true` while `isLoading` is still true and only restores secure-store values asynchronously (`notification_preferences_controller.dart:19-22,49-50`). A user who previously disabled approval or live-activity notifications can therefore still receive early runtime notifications before restore completes, which means the saved preferences do not yet fully control delivery/suppression behavior across relaunches.

sharedStateObservations.observation: `.factory/library/api.md` is now stale about approval targets and can mislead future workers reviewing approval-context behavior.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/library/api.md:23` still says `ApprovalRecordDto.target` only carries generic identifiers like `git.branch_switch`, but the live bridge contract now returns the exact branch via `PendingApprovalAction::target_name()` (`crates/bridge-core/src/lib.rs:580-583,660-663`) and the branch-target fix commit `8c32448` added coverage asserting `approval.target == "release/2026"` (`crates/bridge-core/src/lib.rs:2653-2684`).

summary: Fail. The original branch-target contract/UI gap is fixed at current HEAD, and notification preferences now drive a real live-stream-backed delivery path instead of only UI chrome. But notification delivery is still unreliable because it only surfaces on current thread list/detail routes and can leak disabled notifications during async preference restore after relaunch.
